parties.name: Sarah (Elder Sibling)
parties.story: Dad passed away 6 months ago. He left the family restaurant to both of us equally in his will, but I've been managing it for the last 8 years while David was living in another city. I gave up a corporate career to keep the restaurant alive through COVID. I took a pay cut, worked 70-hour weeks, and personally guaranteed a $200K loan to renovate the kitchen. Now David wants to sell the restaurant and split the proceeds 50/50. That's not fair — half the restaurant's current value is because of MY work. Mom agrees I should get a larger share but won't say it publicly because she doesn't want to pick favorites. I want either 70% ownership or David's share bought out at the pre-renovation value. I also want the family recipes to stay with whoever keeps the restaurant — they're Dad's legacy.

parties.name: David (Younger Sibling)
parties.story: The will says 50/50, and that's what Dad wanted. I appreciate what Sarah has done, but she chose to work at the restaurant — nobody forced her. She also drew a salary the whole time, which I didn't. I moved away because there wasn't room for both of us in the business, and Sarah made it clear she was in charge. I have a family now and I need my inheritance to buy a house. I'm not trying to be unfair — I've offered to let Sarah buy me out at fair market value with a reasonable payment plan. But she wants to value my share at what the restaurant was worth before her renovations, which is $150K less than its actual value. Mom told me privately she thinks the will should be honored as written. I want a fair, independent appraisal and either a buyout at current market value or a sale with 50/50 split.
